facade_description: 278 Augusta Ave presents a bay-and-gable domestic facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 5 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its semi-detached bay-and-gable house-form typology. The facade reads primarily as concrete in a beige/tan brick upper floors with green artificial turf balcony panels on 2nd and 3rd floors, colorful polka-dot painted ground floor (red, yellow, green, purple) palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 3, 3, 2, 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 2 principal entrances. Character-defining facade elements include cornice. HCD note: Each property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, each property is directly associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the ear
